textPlain: Wouldn't be a gary marcus post without congratulating himself for tweeting something that all but confirms a disaster that hasn't happened yet.Anyways, OpenAI is not in profit-seeking mode, and there's no economic incentives to do so right now.
This is so going to get censored from HN
the "If you owe the bank 1M, you have a problem. If you owe the bank 1B, the bank has a problem" adagium scaled up ?
Is Google also deep in the hole with Gemini, or is it just OpenAI that can't make ends meet?

Also important to remember that Google is years ahead of most other AI shops in that they're running on custom silicon. This makes their inference (and maybe training) cheaper then almost any other company. People don't realize this when compared to OpenAI/Anthropic where most folks are utilizing NVIDIA GPUs, Google is completely different in that aspect with their custom TPU platform.

Depends what you mean by deep in the hole. Given how liberal Gemini is with free access it hardly seems like moneymaking is the end goal. Amongst AI labs, I've personally found Deepmind to be consistently the least sensationalist and least concerned with marketing. I think they're doing a great job of commoditising their complement.https://gwern.net/complement
Google has the revenue to cover their spending and then some.
